commit.message:
8379953: trying to resolve build failures on older Macs

I'm not familiar enough with obj c to understand this well, though. Some research suggests this *should* work, and Mac 10.13 was when @"AXHeading" was introduced.

Revert "8379953: reverting previous fix"

This is in response to:
https://github.com/openjdk/jdk/pull/30251#issuecomment-4321677253

This reverts commits 32ec4e54ded7b6968e0e528531711c9d2f24f75b, 938975a2dbaf680af4233bec410e87e1520f247b, 706b0da9cd4a05a49a388e2123be86d413ab31db.

8377938: use NSAccessibilityHeadingRole for AccessibleRole.HEADER

On Mac OS: a "heading" has a special semantic meaning for assistive technologies. It is a more accurate/useful role than "header" (which is a Java construct and doesn't directly map to a Mac OS feature)
